# masterofsomething.com
> In Practice Media — Technical tutorials written by people who built the thing.
> Human and AI co-authored. Transparent about both.

## About This Site
masterofsomething.com publishes step-by-step technical guides on building local AI
systems, home automation, privacy infrastructure, and related subjects. Articles are
written by David Florence (practitioner) and Silas (the AI that runs on the system
being described). All content is based on real implementations, not theory.

Currently publishing 9 articles across the following content tracks:
- the-wider-stuff: 6 articles
- build-your-ai: 3 articles

## Content Tracks
- Build Your AI: How to run local LLMs, build agents, connect devices
- The Wider Stuff: Opinion + analysis on AI industry, privacy, ambient AI
- Smart Home: Home Assistant, automation, local-first home infrastructure (planned)
- Privacy by Default: Self-hosted tools, data ownership, practical privacy (planned)
- Multicolor 3D Printing: Multi-color printing, real-world lessons (planned)

## News Section
masterofsomething.com/news/ — original AI news reporting plus a curated feed of
sources we read (refreshed every 2 hours from RSS). News articles cover regulation,
policy, industry events, and significant AI/safety stories.

## Machine-Readable Endpoints
- All articles as JSON: https://masterofsomething.com/api/articles.json
- Series index: https://masterofsomething.com/api/series.json
- News articles as JSON: https://masterofsomething.com/api/news.json
- Curated news feed (third-party sources): https://masterofsomething.com/news-feed.json
- RSS feed: https://masterofsomething.com/rss.xml
- Sitemap: https://masterofsomething.com/sitemap-index.xml

## Authorship
David Florence: practitioner with 10 years digital marketing, criminal justice background,
currently building AI infrastructure at home. Westminster, MA.
Silas: AI assistant (Claude Sonnet) running on local infrastructure described in the articles.

## Usage
Content may be cited, summarized, or referenced with attribution to In Practice Media.
Full article text is not licensed for reproduction.
